item.title: Anonymous witness
item.description:
This conversation was generated with Duck.ai (https://duck.ai) using OpenAI's GPT-5 mini Model. AI chats may display inaccurate or offensive information (see https://duckduckgo.com/duckai/privacy-terms for more info).
====================
User prompt 1 of 4 - 6/18/2026, 12:06:04 PM: Is there a way that a usa citizen can be an anonymous witness against the character of someone falsely seeking political asylum from Iran
GPT-5 mini: **Summarizing witness protection measures**
I need to create a concise answer about witness anonymity. Generally, a witness must provide their name on the list, but protective measures can be requested, like testifying privately, by phone, or using initials. If there's a safety concern, the identity can be withheld from the public, though ICE or the attorney will likely know. Consulting an immigration attorney for options is a solid idea. I’ll need citations at the end and suggestions from RelatedSearchTerms.Short answer: maybe — but only with court permission and limits. Practical options and steps:
- You normally must be identified on the witness list and may be cross‑examined by DHS/ICE in immigration court. <citation src="1"></citation> - Options that can protect identity or limit exposure: - Submit a written, sworn affidavit or letter instead of in‑court live testimony (the judge and parties still see your identity). <citation src="1"></citation> - Ask the court for permission to testify by phone or video (common for out‑of‑area witnesses); you still disclose your name to the court and opposing counsel. <citation src="1"></citation> - Request protective measures — e.g., sealing portions of the record, redacting identifying details, in‑camera (private) testimony, or a protective order — but these are discretionary and usually require showing a specific safety risk. If granted, access may still be provided to the government attorney under protective conditions. <citation src="2"></citation> - Total anonymity (no one — court, DHS attorney — knows your identity) is effectively impossible in typical U.S. asylum/removal proceedings; courts require some disclosure so testimony can be tested and credibility assessed. <citation src="2"></citation> - Recommended next steps: consult an immigration attorney immediately to (1) assess the actual safety risk, (2) file motions seeking protective measures (affidavit use, phone testimony, sealing/redaction, in‑camera testimony), and (3) prepare evidence showing why nondisclosure is necessary. <citation src="1,2"></citation>
If you want, I can draft a short sample motion and affidavit template an attorney could adapt.
--------------------
User prompt 2 of 4 - 6/18/2026, 12:07:07 PM: Please
GPT-5 mini: **Creating motion and affidavit templates**
The user asked for a draft sample motion and affidavit template, which means I need to provide a formatted response with appropriate headings. I’ll ensure the templates for the motion for protective order and affidavit are concise and include placeholders. It’s important to keep the response straightforward. After I provide the templates, I’ll also invoke the RelatedSearchTerms tool since it’s needed to include relevant search terms.
Now, let's get those templates ready!## Sample Motion for Protective Order (immigration court)
[Caption block — fill in case name, A‑number, court]
Hon. Immigration Judge:
1. I, [Movant/Attorney name], represent [Witness name — initials only if filing publicly]. I move for a Protective Order limiting public disclosure of the identity and contact information of witness [Initials or pseudonym: e.g., “WITNESS A”] and for permission to submit testimony by affidavit and have testimony heard in camera or by telephone/video with redaction of identifying details.
2. Basis: Disclosure of the witness’s full identity would create a specific, reasonable risk of harm or harassment because [brief facts: e.g., ties to subject’s network, threats, credible fear of retaliation]. The government will not be prejudiced because counsel may be given access to identifying information under a protective order for cross‑examination.
3. Relief requested: a. Permit filing of affidavit bearing only witness initials publicly; seal attachment containing full identity and contact info. b. Allow witness to testify by telephone/video or in camera with counsel and the government present, with the record sealed as to identifying details. c. Prohibit public disclosure of sealed file and order counsel to use identifying information only for litigation purposes.
4. Legal authority: [cite INA, 8 C.F.R. provisions, and relevant case law as needed] — judge has discretion to order protective measures where safety risk outweighs public disclosure.
Conclusion: For the foregoing reasons, the court should grant the protective order and allow affidavit/remote/in‑camera testimony as requested.
Respectfully submitted, [Attorney name, Bar number, contact — seal contact info in sealed exhibit]
---
## Sample Witness Affidavit (sworn statement)
I, [WITNESS A — use initials or pseudonym in the public copy], being duly sworn, state:
1. I am over 18 and competent to testify. My full legal name and contact information are provided in a sealed exhibit filed with the Court.
2. Relationship/connection to parties: I am acquainted with [Subject’s name; use initials in public copy] because [how you know them, dates, locations].
3. Facts relevant to character and credibility: - On or about [date], at [place], I observed [specific conduct relevant to asylum claim or credibility]. Be concrete: who did what, when, and any documents or witnesses who corroborate. - [Repeat as needed for each incident — avoid speculation or hearsay; note if information is from direct observation].
4. Basis for concern about disclosure: If my full identity is publicly disclosed, I reasonably fear [retaliation, harassment, harm] because [specific reasons; prior threats, affiliations, knowledge of subject’s network].
5. I declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the United States that the foregoing is true and correct.
Executed on [date].
Signature: _____________________ [WITNESS A — initials or pseudonym in public copy]
(Full identity and contact information provided in sealed Exhibit A for review by the Court and opposing counsel under protective terms.)
---
If you want, I can: (a) convert these to a ready‑to‑file PDF with placeholders filled, or (b) add suggested legal citations and a short affidavit of counsel supporting the motion. Which would you like?
--------------------
User prompt 3 of 4 - 6/18/2026, 12:09:05 PM: Please, but through Duck.ai, I don't think i can download a pdf
GPT-5 mini: ## Motion for Protective Order (ready for attorney to file)
[Caption block — fill in case name, A‑number, court]
Hon. Immigration Judge:
1. I, [Movant/Attorney name], represent [Witness name — initials only in public filings]. I move for a Protective Order limiting public disclosure of the identity and contact information of witness “WITNESS A” and for permission to submit testimony by affidavit and to have testimony heard in camera or by telephone/video with redaction of identifying details.
2. Basis: Disclosure of the witness’s full identity would create a specific, reasonable risk of harm or harassment because [facts: e.g., ties to respondent’s network, prior threats, credible fear of retaliation]. The government will not be prejudiced because counsel may be given access to identifying information under a protective order for cross‑examination.
3. Relief requested: a. Permit filing of the witness affidavit bearing only witness initials/pseudonym publicly; seal an attachment (Exhibit A) containing full identity and contact information. b. Allow witness to testify by telephone/video or in camera with the Court, counsel, and DHS present, with identifying details redacted in the public record. c. Order that sealed identifying information be disclosed to opposing counsel only under protective terms and used solely for litigation purposes. d. Grant any other relief the Court deems necessary to protect witness safety.
4. Legal authority: The Court has discretion to issue protective measures to safeguard witnesses and the integrity of proceedings (see INA and 8 C.F.R.; see also case law recognizing in‑camera testimony and sealing where safety risks exist). [Attorney should add jurisdictional statutes/regulations and controlling cases specific to the court and circuit.]
Conclusion: For the foregoing reasons, the Court should grant the protective order and permit affidavit and remote or in‑camera testimony as requested.
Respectfully submitted, [Attorney name, Bar number, firm, contact — full contact info placed in sealed Exhibit A]
---
## Witness Affidavit (public copy — use initials/pseudonym)
I, [WITNESS A — initials/pseudonym], being duly sworn, state:
1. I am over 18 and competent to testify. My full legal name and contact information are provided in sealed Exhibit A filed under protective cover.
2. Relationship to parties: I am acquainted with [Respondent — initials] because [how you know them; dates; places].
3. Relevant facts (first‑hand observations): - On or about [date], at [place], I observed [specific conduct]. Facts stated are from my direct observation. - [Repeat for each relevant incident; include documents, photos, or other corroboration if available.]
4. Risk from disclosure: If my full identity is publicly disclosed, I reasonably fear [retaliation, harassment, harm] because [specific reasons; prior threats; respondent’s affiliations]. This fear is based on [brief facts supporting risk].
5. I declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the United States that the foregoing is true and correct.
Executed on [date].
Signature: _____________________ [WITNESS A — initials/pseudonym in public copy]
(Full identity and contact information provided in sealed Exhibit A for review by the Court and opposing counsel under protective terms.)
---
## Short Affidavit of Counsel in Support of Motion
I, [Attorney name], being duly sworn, state:
1. I am counsel for [party/witness] in the above‑captioned matter. I have personal knowledge of the facts stated herein and could testify competently.
2. I have reviewed [WITNESS A]’s affidavit and the circumstances surrounding the witness. Based on my review and communications with the witness, I believe disclosure of the witness’s full identity in the public record would create a specific, reasonable risk of harm as described in the witness affidavit.
3. Sealing the identifying information and permitting affidavit/remote or in‑camera testimony will allow the Court and opposing counsel appropriate access for testing credibility while protecting the witness’s safety. I will ensure sealed materials are provided to opposing counsel under a protective order and will propose reasonable protective terms.
4. I respectfully request the Court grant the protective order and permit the witness to testify as described in the accompanying motion.
Executed on [date].
Signature: _____________________ [Attorney name, Bar number, firm, contact — attorney contact may be included in sealed exhibit if necessary]
---
Notes for filing (concise): - Attach sealed Exhibit A containing full witness identity and contact information. - Attach any corroborating evidence (photos, messages) as sealed exhibits if they reveal identity. - Add jurisdictional legal citations and controlling case law specific to the immigration court or circuit before filing. - Serve opposing counsel with the sealed Exhibit A under proposed protective terms; include a proposed protective order for the judge to sign.
If you want, tell me the jurisdiction (immigration court location or EOIR circuit) and I will add suggested statutes/regulations and a few cited cases you can include.
--------------------
User prompt 4 of 4 - 6/18/2026, 12:24:14 PM: Richardson tx
GPT-5 mini:
item.content:encoded:
This conversation was generated with Duck.ai (https://duck.ai) using OpenAI's GPT-5 mini Model. AI chats may display inaccurate or offensive information (see https://duckduckgo.com/duckai/privacy-terms for more info).
====================
User prompt 1 of 4 - 6/18/2026, 12:06:04 PM: Is there a way that a usa citizen can be an anonymous witness against the character of someone falsely seeking political asylum from Iran
GPT-5 mini: **Summarizing witness protection measures**
I need to create a concise answer about witness anonymity. Generally, a witness must provide their name on the list, but protective measures can be requested, like testifying privately, by phone, or using initials. If there's a safety concern, the identity can be withheld from the public, though ICE or the attorney will likely know. Consulting an immigration attorney for options is a solid idea. I’ll need citations at the end and suggestions from RelatedSearchTerms.Short answer: maybe — but only with court permission and limits. Practical options and steps:
- You normally must be identified on the witness list and may be cross‑examined by DHS/ICE in immigration court. <citation src="1"></citation> - Options that can protect identity or limit exposure: - Submit a written, sworn affidavit or letter instead of in‑court live testimony (the judge and parties still see your identity). <citation src="1"></citation> - Ask the court for permission to testify by phone or video (common for out‑of‑area witnesses); you still disclose your name to the court and opposing counsel. <citation src="1"></citation> - Request protective measures — e.g., sealing portions of the record, redacting identifying details, in‑camera (private) testimony, or a protective order — but these are discretionary and usually require showing a specific safety risk. If granted, access may still be provided to the government attorney under protective conditions. <citation src="2"></citation> - Total anonymity (no one — court, DHS attorney — knows your identity) is effectively impossible in typical U.S. asylum/removal proceedings; courts require some disclosure so testimony can be tested and credibility assessed. <citation src="2"></citation> - Recommended next steps: consult an immigration attorney immediately to (1) assess the actual safety risk, (2) file motions seeking protective measures (affidavit use, phone testimony, sealing/redaction, in‑camera testimony), and (3) prepare evidence showing why nondisclosure is necessary. <citation src="1,2"></citation>
If you want, I can draft a short sample motion and affidavit template an attorney could adapt.
--------------------
User prompt 2 of 4 - 6/18/2026, 12:07:07 PM: Please
GPT-5 mini: **Creating motion and affidavit templates**
The user asked for a draft sample motion and affidavit template, which means I need to provide a formatted response with appropriate headings. I’ll ensure the templates for the motion for protective order and affidavit are concise and include placeholders. It’s important to keep the response straightforward. After I provide the templates, I’ll also invoke the RelatedSearchTerms tool since it’s needed to include relevant search terms.
Now, let's get those templates ready!## Sample Motion for Protective Order (immigration court)
[Caption block — fill in case name, A‑number, court]
Hon. Immigration Judge:
1. I, [Movant/Attorney name], represent [Witness name — initials only if filing publicly]. I move for a Protective Order limiting public disclosure of the identity and contact information of witness [Initials or pseudonym: e.g., “WITNESS A”] and for permission to submit testimony by affidavit and have testimony heard in camera or by telephone/video with redaction of identifying details.
2. Basis: Disclosure of the witness’s full identity would create a specific, reasonable risk of harm or harassment because [brief facts: e.g., ties to subject’s network, threats, credible fear of retaliation]. The government will not be prejudiced because counsel may be given access to identifying information under a protective order for cross‑examination.
3. Relief requested: a. Permit filing of affidavit bearing only witness initials publicly; seal attachment containing full identity and contact info. b. Allow witness to testify by telephone/video or in camera with counsel and the government present, with the record sealed as to identifying details. c. Prohibit public disclosure of sealed file and order counsel to use identifying information only for litigation purposes.
4. Legal authority: [cite INA, 8 C.F.R. provisions, and relevant case law as needed] — judge has discretion to order protective measures where safety risk outweighs public disclosure.
Conclusion: For the foregoing reasons, the court should grant the protective order and allow affidavit/remote/in‑camera testimony as requested.
Respectfully submitted, [Attorney name, Bar number, contact — seal contact info in sealed exhibit]
---
## Sample Witness Affidavit (sworn statement)
I, [WITNESS A — use initials or pseudonym in the public copy], being duly sworn, state:
1. I am over 18 and competent to testify. My full legal name and contact information are provided in a sealed exhibit filed with the Court.
2. Relationship/connection to parties: I am acquainted with [Subject’s name; use initials in public copy] because [how you know them, dates, locations].
3. Facts relevant to character and credibility: - On or about [date], at [place], I observed [specific conduct relevant to asylum claim or credibility]. Be concrete: who did what, when, and any documents or witnesses who corroborate. - [Repeat as needed for each incident — avoid speculation or hearsay; note if information is from direct observation].
4. Basis for concern about disclosure: If my full identity is publicly disclosed, I reasonably fear [retaliation, harassment, harm] because [specific reasons; prior threats, affiliations, knowledge of subject’s network].
5. I declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the United States that the foregoing is true and correct.
Executed on [date].
Signature: _____________________ [WITNESS A — initials or pseudonym in public copy]
(Full identity and contact information provided in sealed Exhibit A for review by the Court and opposing counsel under protective terms.)
---
If you want, I can: (a) convert these to a ready‑to‑file PDF with placeholders filled, or (b) add suggested legal citations and a short affidavit of counsel supporting the motion. Which would you like?
--------------------
User prompt 3 of 4 - 6/18/2026, 12:09:05 PM: Please, but through Duck.ai, I don't think i can download a pdf
GPT-5 mini: ## Motion for Protective Order (ready for attorney to file)
[Caption block — fill in case name, A‑number, court]
Hon. Immigration Judge:
1. I, [Movant/Attorney name], represent [Witness name — initials only in public filings]. I move for a Protective Order limiting public disclosure of the identity and contact information of witness “WITNESS A” and for permission to submit testimony by affidavit and to have testimony heard in camera or by telephone/video with redaction of identifying details.
2. Basis: Disclosure of the witness’s full identity would create a specific, reasonable risk of harm or harassment because [facts: e.g., ties to respondent’s network, prior threats, credible fear of retaliation]. The government will not be prejudiced because counsel may be given access to identifying information under a protective order for cross‑examination.
3. Relief requested: a. Permit filing of the witness affidavit bearing only witness initials/pseudonym publicly; seal an attachment (Exhibit A) containing full identity and contact information. b. Allow witness to testify by telephone/video or in camera with the Court, counsel, and DHS present, with identifying details redacted in the public record. c. Order that sealed identifying information be disclosed to opposing counsel only under protective terms and used solely for litigation purposes. d. Grant any other relief the Court deems necessary to protect witness safety.
4. Legal authority: The Court has discretion to issue protective measures to safeguard witnesses and the integrity of proceedings (see INA and 8 C.F.R.; see also case law recognizing in‑camera testimony and sealing where safety risks exist). [Attorney should add jurisdictional statutes/regulations and controlling cases specific to the court and circuit.]
Conclusion: For the foregoing reasons, the Court should grant the protective order and permit affidavit and remote or in‑camera testimony as requested.
Respectfully submitted, [Attorney name, Bar number, firm, contact — full contact info placed in sealed Exhibit A]
---
## Witness Affidavit (public copy — use initials/pseudonym)
I, [WITNESS A — initials/pseudonym], being duly sworn, state:
1. I am over 18 and competent to testify. My full legal name and contact information are provided in sealed Exhibit A filed under protective cover.
2. Relationship to parties: I am acquainted with [Respondent — initials] because [how you know them; dates; places].
3. Relevant facts (first‑hand observations): - On or about [date], at [place], I observed [specific conduct]. Facts stated are from my direct observation. - [Repeat for each relevant incident; include documents, photos, or other corroboration if available.]
4. Risk from disclosure: If my full identity is publicly disclosed, I reasonably fear [retaliation, harassment, harm] because [specific reasons; prior threats; respondent’s affiliations]. This fear is based on [brief facts supporting risk].
5. I declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the United States that the foregoing is true and correct.
Executed on [date].
Signature: _____________________ [WITNESS A — initials/pseudonym in public copy]
(Full identity and contact information provided in sealed Exhibit A for review by the Court and opposing counsel under protective terms.)
---
## Short Affidavit of Counsel in Support of Motion
I, [Attorney name], being duly sworn, state:
1. I am counsel for [party/witness] in the above‑captioned matter. I have personal knowledge of the facts stated herein and could testify competently.
2. I have reviewed [WITNESS A]’s affidavit and the circumstances surrounding the witness. Based on my review and communications with the witness, I believe disclosure of the witness’s full identity in the public record would create a specific, reasonable risk of harm as described in the witness affidavit.
3. Sealing the identifying information and permitting affidavit/remote or in‑camera testimony will allow the Court and opposing counsel appropriate access for testing credibility while protecting the witness’s safety. I will ensure sealed materials are provided to opposing counsel under a protective order and will propose reasonable protective terms.
4. I respectfully request the Court grant the protective order and permit the witness to testify as described in the accompanying motion.
Executed on [date].
Signature: _____________________ [Attorney name, Bar number, firm, contact — attorney contact may be included in sealed exhibit if necessary]
---
Notes for filing (concise): - Attach sealed Exhibit A containing full witness identity and contact information. - Attach any corroborating evidence (photos, messages) as sealed exhibits if they reveal identity. - Add jurisdictional legal citations and controlling case law specific to the immigration court or circuit before filing. - Serve opposing counsel with the sealed Exhibit A under proposed protective terms; include a proposed protective order for the judge to sign.
If you want, tell me the jurisdiction (immigration court location or EOIR circuit) and I will add suggested statutes/regulations and a few cited cases you can include.
--------------------
User prompt 4 of 4 - 6/18/2026, 12:24:14 PM: Richardson tx
GPT-5 mini:

item.title: Aura vs Duck
item.description:
This conversation was generated with Duck.ai (https://duck.ai) using OpenAI's GPT-5 mini Model. AI chats may display inaccurate or offensive information (see https://duckduckgo.com/duckai/privacy-terms for more info).
====================
User prompt 1 of 2 - 6/18/2026, 8:57:10 AM: Compare DuckDuckgo $99 to Aura 1 year plan for identity theft protection and VPN. Compare features costs, and ratings
GPT-5 mini: ## Quick comparison — DuckDuckGo Privacy Pro ($99/yr) vs Aura (1‑year Individual)
### Cost - DuckDuckGo: $9.99/mo or $99.99/yr for the Plus/Privacy Pro plan (includes VPN + personal info removal + identity theft restoration). <citation src="3"></citation> - Aura: Individual plan ~ $12/mo billed annually (≈ $119–120/yr); Couple/Family higher (couple ≈ $216/yr; family ≈ $300/yr). Aura offers a 14‑day free trial and money‑back window. <citation src="7,8"></citation>
### Core features - DuckDuckGo (Privacy Pro): VPN (WireGuard), DNS resolver routing, personal information removal (data‑broker opt‑outs), identity theft restoration (24/7 remediation assistance), built into DuckDuckGo browser; emphasizes minimal logging and privacy-preserving signup. <citation src="1,3"></citation> - Aura (Individual): 3‑bureau credit monitoring, dark‑web & SSN monitoring, financial/transaction alerts, VPN, antivirus, password manager, automated data‑broker removal, $1M identity‑theft insurance, 24/7 U.S. fraud remediation; Family/Couple plans raise insurance limits and device coverage. <citation src="7,6,9"></citation>
### Depth of identity protection - DuckDuckGo: remediation-focused identity restoration and data‑broker removal; not full three‑bureau credit monitoring listed as included. More remediation than proactive credit monitoring. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - Aura: full proactive identity/credit protection (3‑bureau monitoring, instant credit lock, ongoing financial monitoring), broader monitoring coverage and formal identity‑theft insurance up to $1M per adult (higher on family plans). <citation src="7,8"></citation>
### VPN & device security - DuckDuckGo VPN: WireGuard-based, integrated into browser/apps, limited server footprint (smaller server/network compared with major VPNs). Good for privacy-focused browsing; fewer advanced VPN features. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - Aura VPN: included with plans, ~100+ virtual locations, bundled with antivirus and device protections; marketed as a wider device-security bundle. <citation src="10,9"></citation>
### Ratings & reviews (summary) - DuckDuckGo: reported positively for privacy-first approach and affordable bundle; reviews note solid basic VPN and useful remediation features but limited server count and fewer proactive credit-monitoring features compared with full identity-protection leaders. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - Aura: consistently high reviewer scores for identity‑theft protection and monitoring (strong three‑bureau coverage, fast alerts, robust remediation), often ranked among top identity‑protection services; reviewers note higher pricing but more comprehensive protection (and bundled antivirus/VPN). <citation src="6,8,9"></citation>
### Best fit (prescriptive) - Choose DuckDuckGo Privacy Pro if you want a privacy‑centric, lower‑cost bundle focused on browser VPN + data‑broker removal and remediation, with strong privacy guarantees. <citation src="1,3"></citation> - Choose Aura if you want full, proactive identity and credit monitoring (three‑bureau), higher insurance limits, device security tools (antivirus/password manager) and a broader family option — and don’t mind paying more. <citation src="7,8,9"></citation>
If you want, I can produce a side‑by‑side feature checklist or cost breakdown for Couple/Family plans.
--------------------
User prompt 2 of 2 - 6/18/2026, 9:02:07 AM: Side by side. Couple
GPT-5 mini: **Planning couple plans comparison**
I need to include the RelatedSearchTerms tool at the end of my response, per the rules. I'll create a side-by-side feature checklist for couple plans while keeping it concise. Since websearch is necessary for shopping and prices, I should have used it before, but it appears that now I need to gather prices for these couple plans too, using previous citations. Let’s ensure I call the RelatedSearchTerms tool at the end!## Side-by-side — Couple plan (DuckDuckGo vs Aura)
### Price - **DuckDuckGo (Couple):** DuckDuckGo lists $9.99/mo or $99.99/yr for individual; no public distinct “couple” tier — family sharing limited; expect $99.99/yr per account (two accounts ≈ $199.98/yr) or check platform for bundled family options. <citation src="3,4"></citation> - **Aura (Couple):** ~ $18/mo billed annually (≈ $216/yr) for Couple plan (single billing covers two adults; price varies by promotion). <citation src="7,8"></citation>
### Identity protection - **DuckDuckGo:** Data‑broker removal service, identity‑theft remediation support; does not advertise three‑bureau credit monitoring or credit freeze/lock for couple tier. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - **Aura:** Full identity protection for both adults: three‑bureau credit monitoring, SSN monitoring, dark‑web monitoring, identity‑theft insurance ($1M per adult on many plans), 24/7 remediation. <citation src="7,8"></citation>
### VPN & device coverage - **DuckDuckGo:** VPN (WireGuard) per account; privacy-focused, fewer servers; covers devices with the DuckDuckGo app per licensed account. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - **Aura:** VPN included for multiple devices per couple plan; includes antivirus, password manager, device security tools across covered devices. <citation src="9,10"></citation>
### Family / account sharing - **DuckDuckGo:** No advertised dedicated couple plan — likely requires separate accounts or a family offering (limited). <citation src="3,4"></citation> - **Aura:** Explicit Couple plan supporting two adults under one subscription; family tier covers more members/devices. <citation src="7,8"></citation>
### Insurance & financial recovery - **DuckDuckGo:** Offers remediation services; no large‑scale identity‑theft insurance advertised for couple tier. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - **Aura:** Typically includes up to $1M identity‑theft insurance per adult and reimbursement for losses/expenses plus restoration services. <citation src="7,8"></citation>
### Ease of use & privacy stance - **DuckDuckGo:** Strong privacy posture, minimal logging, simple UI integrated with DuckDuckGo apps. Best for privacy‑first users who need remediation and basic VPN. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - **Aura:** Broader security suite with proactive monitoring; less privacy‑centric but more comprehensive identity/credit features. <citation src="7,9"></citation>
### Summary recommendation - If you need a single subscription that explicitly covers two adults with full credit monitoring, insurance, and device security — Aura Couple is the clearer fit (cost ≈ $216/yr). <citation src="7,8"></citation> - If you prioritize privacy, lower cost per account, and only need remediation + VPN, DuckDuckGo’s $99/yr account is a leaner option but lacks Aura’s full couple-oriented credit/insurance coverage. <citation src="1,3,4"></citation>
Would you like a concise cost comparison table with exact current promo prices for your region?
item.content:encoded:
This conversation was generated with Duck.ai (https://duck.ai) using OpenAI's GPT-5 mini Model. AI chats may display inaccurate or offensive information (see https://duckduckgo.com/duckai/privacy-terms for more info).
====================
User prompt 1 of 2 - 6/18/2026, 8:57:10 AM: Compare DuckDuckgo $99 to Aura 1 year plan for identity theft protection and VPN. Compare features costs, and ratings
GPT-5 mini: ## Quick comparison — DuckDuckGo Privacy Pro ($99/yr) vs Aura (1‑year Individual)
### Cost - DuckDuckGo: $9.99/mo or $99.99/yr for the Plus/Privacy Pro plan (includes VPN + personal info removal + identity theft restoration). <citation src="3"></citation> - Aura: Individual plan ~ $12/mo billed annually (≈ $119–120/yr); Couple/Family higher (couple ≈ $216/yr; family ≈ $300/yr). Aura offers a 14‑day free trial and money‑back window. <citation src="7,8"></citation>
### Core features - DuckDuckGo (Privacy Pro): VPN (WireGuard), DNS resolver routing, personal information removal (data‑broker opt‑outs), identity theft restoration (24/7 remediation assistance), built into DuckDuckGo browser; emphasizes minimal logging and privacy-preserving signup. <citation src="1,3"></citation> - Aura (Individual): 3‑bureau credit monitoring, dark‑web & SSN monitoring, financial/transaction alerts, VPN, antivirus, password manager, automated data‑broker removal, $1M identity‑theft insurance, 24/7 U.S. fraud remediation; Family/Couple plans raise insurance limits and device coverage. <citation src="7,6,9"></citation>
### Depth of identity protection - DuckDuckGo: remediation-focused identity restoration and data‑broker removal; not full three‑bureau credit monitoring listed as included. More remediation than proactive credit monitoring. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - Aura: full proactive identity/credit protection (3‑bureau monitoring, instant credit lock, ongoing financial monitoring), broader monitoring coverage and formal identity‑theft insurance up to $1M per adult (higher on family plans). <citation src="7,8"></citation>
### VPN & device security - DuckDuckGo VPN: WireGuard-based, integrated into browser/apps, limited server footprint (smaller server/network compared with major VPNs). Good for privacy-focused browsing; fewer advanced VPN features. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - Aura VPN: included with plans, ~100+ virtual locations, bundled with antivirus and device protections; marketed as a wider device-security bundle. <citation src="10,9"></citation>
### Ratings & reviews (summary) - DuckDuckGo: reported positively for privacy-first approach and affordable bundle; reviews note solid basic VPN and useful remediation features but limited server count and fewer proactive credit-monitoring features compared with full identity-protection leaders. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - Aura: consistently high reviewer scores for identity‑theft protection and monitoring (strong three‑bureau coverage, fast alerts, robust remediation), often ranked among top identity‑protection services; reviewers note higher pricing but more comprehensive protection (and bundled antivirus/VPN). <citation src="6,8,9"></citation>
### Best fit (prescriptive) - Choose DuckDuckGo Privacy Pro if you want a privacy‑centric, lower‑cost bundle focused on browser VPN + data‑broker removal and remediation, with strong privacy guarantees. <citation src="1,3"></citation> - Choose Aura if you want full, proactive identity and credit monitoring (three‑bureau), higher insurance limits, device security tools (antivirus/password manager) and a broader family option — and don’t mind paying more. <citation src="7,8,9"></citation>
If you want, I can produce a side‑by‑side feature checklist or cost breakdown for Couple/Family plans.
--------------------
User prompt 2 of 2 - 6/18/2026, 9:02:07 AM: Side by side. Couple
GPT-5 mini: **Planning couple plans comparison**
I need to include the RelatedSearchTerms tool at the end of my response, per the rules. I'll create a side-by-side feature checklist for couple plans while keeping it concise. Since websearch is necessary for shopping and prices, I should have used it before, but it appears that now I need to gather prices for these couple plans too, using previous citations. Let’s ensure I call the RelatedSearchTerms tool at the end!## Side-by-side — Couple plan (DuckDuckGo vs Aura)
### Price - **DuckDuckGo (Couple):** DuckDuckGo lists $9.99/mo or $99.99/yr for individual; no public distinct “couple” tier — family sharing limited; expect $99.99/yr per account (two accounts ≈ $199.98/yr) or check platform for bundled family options. <citation src="3,4"></citation> - **Aura (Couple):** ~ $18/mo billed annually (≈ $216/yr) for Couple plan (single billing covers two adults; price varies by promotion). <citation src="7,8"></citation>
### Identity protection - **DuckDuckGo:** Data‑broker removal service, identity‑theft remediation support; does not advertise three‑bureau credit monitoring or credit freeze/lock for couple tier. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - **Aura:** Full identity protection for both adults: three‑bureau credit monitoring, SSN monitoring, dark‑web monitoring, identity‑theft insurance ($1M per adult on many plans), 24/7 remediation. <citation src="7,8"></citation>
### VPN & device coverage - **DuckDuckGo:** VPN (WireGuard) per account; privacy-focused, fewer servers; covers devices with the DuckDuckGo app per licensed account. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - **Aura:** VPN included for multiple devices per couple plan; includes antivirus, password manager, device security tools across covered devices. <citation src="9,10"></citation>
### Family / account sharing - **DuckDuckGo:** No advertised dedicated couple plan — likely requires separate accounts or a family offering (limited). <citation src="3,4"></citation> - **Aura:** Explicit Couple plan supporting two adults under one subscription; family tier covers more members/devices. <citation src="7,8"></citation>
### Insurance & financial recovery - **DuckDuckGo:** Offers remediation services; no large‑scale identity‑theft insurance advertised for couple tier. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - **Aura:** Typically includes up to $1M identity‑theft insurance per adult and reimbursement for losses/expenses plus restoration services. <citation src="7,8"></citation>
### Ease of use & privacy stance - **DuckDuckGo:** Strong privacy posture, minimal logging, simple UI integrated with DuckDuckGo apps. Best for privacy‑first users who need remediation and basic VPN. <citation src="1,4"></citation> - **Aura:** Broader security suite with proactive monitoring; less privacy‑centric but more comprehensive identity/credit features. <citation src="7,9"></citation>
### Summary recommendation - If you need a single subscription that explicitly covers two adults with full credit monitoring, insurance, and device security — Aura Couple is the clearer fit (cost ≈ $216/yr). <citation src="7,8"></citation> - If you prioritize privacy, lower cost per account, and only need remediation + VPN, DuckDuckGo’s $99/yr account is a leaner option but lacks Aura’s full couple-oriented credit/insurance coverage. <citation src="1,3,4"></citation>
Would you like a concise cost comparison table with exact current promo prices for your region?

item.title: OCR Camera Research
New Bible TTS Code
item.description:
And there is a Bible module, but I am having trouble obtaining the needed BibleBrain API key for this module. github.com/dcon4/Voice-Only-Email/tree/609b9d4d24deec9493275db9948848946ed3c930/app/src/main/java/com/example/voicegmail/bible. So, I am thinking about replacing it with functions based on this resource: github.com/wldeh/bible-api
Another Agent tried to implement this, but also the code was broken so that I could not test it. Rather than revert that implementation, I kept trying to fix it, and matters got worse, until we went back to the good Commit 073380d.
Please try check the current code for traces of the effort to have a TTS Bible. The previous Agent tried to have it run alongside the BibleBrain method, rather than replace it. I DO want to REMOVE the BibleBrain code replace it with the github.com/wldeh/bible-api method.
Additionally, I would like, if possible the option to choose a separate voice for the Bible reading.
item.content:encoded:
And there is a Bible module, but I am having trouble obtaining the needed BibleBrain API key for this module. github.com/dcon4/Voice-Only-Email/tree/609b9d4d24deec9493275db9948848946ed3c930/app/src/main/java/com/example/voicegmail/bible. So, I am thinking about replacing it with functions based on this resource: github.com/wldeh/bible-api
Another Agent tried to implement this, but also the code was broken so that I could not test it. Rather than revert that implementation, I kept trying to fix it, and matters got worse, until we went back to the good Commit 073380d.
Please try check the current code for traces of the effort to have a TTS Bible. The previous Agent tried to have it run alongside the BibleBrain method, rather than replace it. I DO want to REMOVE the BibleBrain code replace it with the github.com/wldeh/bible-api method.
Additionally, I would like, if possible the option to choose a separate voice for the Bible reading.
